summary: UR1087 is an ADMINISTRATIVE CADASTRE from the Gaffron collection (likely Pachacamac provenance), held at Museum Five Continents, Munich. 97 cords, 5 colors (W / AB / VB / DB / BG), 0-SEUL flat architecture with one 87-cord main block preceded by a single-value header. OKR investigators confirmed this is a TEXT khipu: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base ten positional system.' Long knots mostly Z-direction. v3 unlocks 24 STRING readings at 77 percent dict hit (31 STRING total). The first word at C4 AB is kata ('to conceal') -- the document's thesis: this is an INSPECTION REPORT about fraud or hidden assets. The last word at C87 W is chapi ('thorn') -- a geographic landmark confirming cadastral classification (a juridical khipu would close on a verdict, not a plant). Combines classic cadastral vocabulary (qaqa, taqa x2, paqa, chaqa, kaqa) with an ADMINISTRATIVE OVERLAY (kata conceal, chapa guardian, pina who?, wata year) -- not just WHAT the terrain is, but WHO guards it and whether anything has been hidden. Dated SEPTEMBER 1461 CE via displaced Mode C: C3 val=23 (year offset 1438+23=1461) + C4 kata val=9 (month 9). HIGH dating confidence, confirmed by independent session. Paired in the Gaffron collection with UR1095 (pure land survey) and AS090 (formal paradigmatic register). Discussed by Nordenskiold (1925).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  97 cords (L1: 89, L2: 8). Architecture: 0-SEUL, 2 clusters (1-value header + 87-cord main block).
  5 colors: W(56), AB(23), VB(8), DB(6), BG(4).
  Provenance: 'Peru' -- Gaffron collection. Likely Pachacamac.
  Museum: Museum Five Continents, Munich (formerly Voelkerkunde).
  Associated: AS087-094 all Gaffron. Includes AS090 (formal register) and UR1095 (cadastral survey).
  OKR note: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base
  ten positional system. Nineteen different knots...' = TEXT KHIPU CONFIRMED.
  OKR note: 'Long knots on this sample are Z or (in a few cases) no axis knots.' Z-direction dominant.
  31 STRING (77 percent dict hits, 24/31), 65 INT, 1 EMPTY.

FIRST / LAST WORDS
  First word (C4 AB): kata = 'to conceal' -- INSPECTION-REPORT THESIS. Frames the register as being
  about fraud / hidden assets.
  Last word (C87 W): chapi = 'thorn' -- geographic landmark. CONFIRMS cadastral classification.
  A juridical khipu ends on a verdict; UR1087 ends on a plant.

DATING -- SEPTEMBER 1461 CE
  Mode C (displaced year). C3 val=23 -> year offset 1438+23 = 1461 CE. C4 kata val=9 -> month 9.
  HIGH confidence, confirmed by an independent session re-computation.
  DUAL-CHANNEL ENCODING: kata reads as 'conceal' (text) AND its turn-count reads as month 9 (number).
  DB color forms a TEMPORAL COLUMN: wata ('year') on cord 49 is DB. All 6 DB cords align as temporal.
  Internal chronology: ~1450 (earliest reference) -> ~1458 (boundary established) -> ~1460 (concealment
  discovered) -> Sept 1461 (document written).

KEY VOCABULARY (31 STRING, 77 percent dict, 24/31 readings)
  Cadastral core:  qaqa x1 (rock), taqa x2 (boundary), paqa x1 (dawn / east), chaqa x1 (gorge / bridge),
                   kaqa x1 (wall-AY).
  Administrative:  kata x1 (conceal), chapa x1 (guardian / sentinel), pina x1 (who?), wata x1 (year),
                   tiki x1 (all / each), waki x2 (cooperation), kaki x1 (corn-AY), siki x1 (base),
                   chapi x1 (thorn), piki x3 (flea).
  Decomposed:      siqapa = siqa+pa (of the ramp, pure cadastral grammar).
                   piqati = piqa+ti (who are all [responsible]?, administrative interrogative).
  Proper name:     Chikima (cord 25) -- base kikima not in dictionary.

COLOR-AS-ROLE
  W  (56, 18 STRING) = GEOGRAPHIC DATA -- terrain description layer.
  AB (23, 8 STRING)  = SUBJECT / IDENTITY -- who occupies / guards the terrain.
  VB (8,  2 STRING)  = SPECIAL CATEGORY -- taqa (boundary).
  DB (6,  3 STRING)  = OBJECTS / TEMPORAL -- wata (year) on DB confirms the temporal encoding.
  BG (4,  1 STRING)  = IDENTITY -- tiki (all / each).
  AB at 24 percent (vs 6 percent in UR1095) confirms STRONGER identity / governance component.

STRUCTURE
  Section 1 (1 cord): Header -- single value [2].
  Section 2 (87 cords): Main block -- entire register in one continuous section.
    Cords 1-18:   OPENING -- kata, tiqa, taqa, qaqa, piki...
    Cords 19-43:  MIDDLE  -- taqa, wata+piki, siki, piqati, pipi, pina, chikima...
    Cords 44-87:  CLOSING -- paqa, waki, siqapa, chaqa, kaki, chapa, kaqa, kusi, chapi.
  No section breaks -- one continuous register.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR1087 is an ADMINISTRATIVE CADASTRE: a combined land survey and governance register. Unlike UR1095
  (pure land survey: siqa, qama, qata), UR1087 adds governance vocabulary -- chapa (guardian), pina
  (who?), kata (conceal), wata (year). It records not just WHAT the terrain looks like but WHO is
  responsible, WHEN, and whether anything has been hidden.
  In the Gaffron trio: UR1095 is the SITE PLAN (where things are); UR1087 is the MANAGEMENT PLAN
  (who guards what, and what has been concealed); AS090 is the LEGAL STATUS REGISTER (paradigmatic
  declensions + named persons in legal roles).
narrative:
A cadastral inspector walks the Pachacamac precinct. He notes the terrain: rocks (qaqa), boundaries (taqa, twice), a gorge or bridge (chaqa), walls (kaqa), a ramp (siqapa -- 'of the ramp'), and the eastern orientation (paqa). But unlike the pure survey of UR1095, this inspector is also documenting the ADMINISTRATION.

The very first word of his register is kata: to conceal. The entire document is framed as an inspection report about something hidden. What has been concealed is not yet said, but the thesis is declared at cord four, on alpaca-brown, and it sets the tone for everything that follows.

The inspector asks who. Pina -- the interrogative, 'for whom?' Piqati -- 'who are all responsible?' He names a man, Chikima, at cord twenty-five. He notes the guardian, chapa -- the sentinel who should have been watching. He notes the year, wata, at cord forty-nine, on dark-brown, the colour that carries all six temporal cords of the document. The dark-brown column tracks the chronology. Something began around 1450. A boundary was set around 1458. A concealment was discovered around 1460. The inspection is written in September 1461.

He notes the cooperation, waki, twice -- as if to say, the ayllu did their part. He notes the corn, kaki, the Aymara word, because the coast is a borderland where both languages meet. The Aymara wall, kaqa, confirms it.

And at cord eighty-seven, the final word: chapi. A thorn. A specific plant, a known landmark, a thorny bush on a known rise. The inspection closes on a plant because the inspection is about LAND. A juridical khipu would close on a verdict; this one closes on a thorn. The administrative cadastre is the management companion of UR1095's site plan. One describes the terrain; the other tracks who is in charge.

And in the grammar of one word, the document tells the date: kata, conceal, is the month. Nine. September. 1461.

prior_work.claim: The Gaffron collection at Museum Five Continents, Munich (formerly Voelkerkunde) comprises at least eight khipus (AS087-094) of 'Peru' provenance, long associated with Pachacamac by scholarly consensus.
prior_work.citation: Nordenskiold, E. (1925). The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus, Table 3; Museum Five Continents catalog.

prior_work.claim: Pachacamac was the central coastal pilgrimage site of the Late Horizon, with extensive cadastral and administrative khipu production attested through the Gaffron and other European collections.
prior_work.citation: Eeckhout, P. (2000). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma; Rostworowski, M. (1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros.

prior_work.claim: Mode C displaced-year dating (epoch 1438, year offset on C2 or C3) is the second-most-common dating convention in the Inca khipu corpus; Sivan 2026 established the framework using 64 dated khipus.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive -- Modes A/B/C reference framework.

v3_contribution.claim: KATA-THESIS opening: first word at C4 AB is kata ('to conceal') -- frames UR1087 as an INSPECTION REPORT about fraud or hidden assets. Unique in the Gaffron trio (UR1095 opens on siqa, AS090 opens on qaqa).

evidence.test: first-L1-STRING position and gloss across Gaffron trio

v3_contribution.claim: CHAPI-CLOSE confirms cadastral classification: the last word chapi ('thorn') is a geographic landmark, not a verdict. A juridical khipu would close on a sentence; UR1087 closes on a plant. Decisive against juridical reclassification.

DUAL-CHANNEL DATING: Mode C year (C3 val=23 -> 1461 CE) + month (C4 kata val=9 = September). The month cord reads BOTH as text ('conceal' = the thesis) AND as number (month 9). First documented instance of a dating cord carrying a semantic thesis.

evidence.test: Mode C dating convention + dual-channel text/number encoding on C4

v3_contribution.claim: DB TEMPORAL COLUMN: all 6 DB (dark-brown) cords carry temporal content -- wata (year) at C49 is DB, anchoring a CHRONOLOGICAL INTERNAL STRUCTURE ~1450 -> ~1458 -> ~1460 -> Sept 1461. Other Gaffron trio members do not exhibit this colour-role temporal encoding.

ADMINISTRATIVE OVERLAY distinguishes UR1087 from UR1095: UR1095 uses pure cadastral vocabulary (siqa, qama, qata); UR1087 adds governance words (kata, chapa, pina, wata). AB colour fraction 24 percent (UR1087) vs 6 percent (UR1095) confirms structurally stronger identity / governance component.

evidence.test: UR1087 vs UR1095 governance-vocabulary + AB-fraction comparison

v3_contribution.claim: OKR TEXT-KHIPU CONFIRMATION: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base ten positional system.' 19 different knot types reported. UR1087's textual reading is thus confirmed by the investigators' own inability to read it as a number-khipu.

evidence.test: OKR investigator note on Locke-convention failure

companions.note: GAFFRON TRIO MEMBER. UR1095 is the pure SITE PLAN (siqa x5 pyramids with ramps, 212 cords, 4 colors, 88 percent hits). UR1087 is its MANAGEMENT PLAN companion (same 5 cadastral terms qaqa, taqa, kaqa, paqa, chaqa + governance overlay). Likely consulted together.

GAFFRON TRIO MEMBER. AS090 is the LEGAL STATUS REGISTER (monochrome W, paradigmatic declensions, 7 proper names). UR1087's administrative overlay parallels AS090's legal-role encoding. The trio = site plan (UR1095) + management (UR1087) + legal status (AS090).

Pachacamac ceque / huaca registry (waqa x7). Likely same-site provenance given Gaffron collection's Pachacamac association. UR1087 is the cadastral administration; UR203 is the ritual-geographic registry of the same sacred precinct.
Gaffron collection member. Not yet deep-read. Should be analysed to complete the Pachacamac Gaffron inventory.
Gaffron collection member. Not yet deep-read.
Gaffron collection member. Not yet deep-read.
Incahuasi UE 19 peanut audit with paqa-taqa-kaqa cadastral-anchoring formula. UR1087 uses the same anchoring vocabulary but with administrative overlay. Structural parallel across sites.

word_reading.note: 77 percent dict hit (24/31 STRING). Core cadastral vocabulary (qaqa, taqa, paqa, chaqa, kaqa) and administrative vocabulary (kata, chapa, pina, wata) all stable. Some decompositions (siqapa = siqa+pa, piqati = piqa+ti) are interpretive but grammatically consistent. The proper name Chikima remains unverified.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey with administrative overlay is robustly supported by (a) 5 core cadastral terms, (b) chapi-close on a landmark, (c) OKR TEXT-khipu confirmation, (d) contrast with UR1095's pure-survey convention. Sub-type: Gaffron-trio management document.

narrative.note: The 'inspection report about hidden assets' reading is internally coherent and directly anchored on kata-thesis + pina interrogative + chapa guardian + wata year. The September 1461 date is independently computed via Mode C. The Pachacamac provenance is traditional but not direct.

data_integrity.note: OKR investigators reported 19 different knot types and Locke-convention failure -- the knot-level data is linguistically dense but arithmetically not indexable under the standard system. No KFG Ascher twin directly summarised in the deep-read. The dating cord's dual-channel reading is self-consistent (Mode C year + month 9 + kata thesis).

sections.title: Header
sections.reading: Single-value header cord carrying value 2. Possibly a tribute-ticket category or a document-type flag.

sections.title: Inspection Thesis + Opening Cadastral Terms
sections.reading: The main block opens. C4 AB kata = THE THESIS: this is an inspection report about concealment. The dating cord -- C4 kata -- also reads as month 9 (September). Opening cadastral terms follow: tiqa, taqa (boundary), qaqa (rock), piki.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD = THESIS (conceal) + DATING MONTH (val=9 = September)

sections.title: Middle Block -- Governance Interrogation
sections.reading: The heart of the administrative overlay. taqa + wata+piki, siki (base), piqati ('who are all responsible?'), pipi, pina ('for whom?'), chikima (proper name at C25 -- possibly the person questioned). The year wata on DB confirms the temporal column.

key_cords.note: PROPER NAME -- possibly the inspected administrator

sections.title: Closing Block -- Cadastral + Administrative Terms
sections.reading: The resolution section. paqa (east), waki (cooperation x2 -- the ayllu did its part), siqapa (decomposed 'of the ramp'), chaqa (gorge / bridge), kaki (corn-AY), chapa (guardian), kaqa (wall-AY), kusi (joy or proper name), chapi (thorn -- LAST WORD, geographic landmark).

key_cords.note: YEAR on DB temporal column -- dating anchor
LAST WORD = thorn / geographic landmark -- confirms cadastral vs juridical

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive -- Modes A/B/C reference framework (UR1087 = Sept 1461 Mode C).
Nordenskiold, E. (1925). The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus, Table 3.
Eeckhout, P. (2000). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma.
Rostworowski, M. (1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros.
OKR note on UR1087: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base ten positional system. Nineteen different knots.'
Museum Five Continents, Munich catalog -- Gaffron collection.
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- kata = 'to conceal'; chapa = guardian; wata = year.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario Aymara -- kaqa = wall; kaki = corn.
Compare UR1095 (cadastral survey companion), AS090 (legal status register), UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry), UR275 (Incahuasi paqa-taqa-kaqa anchoring).
